user_query:
I saved a few old announcement pages locally so I don’t have to rely on live sites. Please scan every .html file under input/sites/ (recursively), extract event-like entries, and build a small index of items that mention my friend Mr. Ma Hanbao by name. Requirements: 1) Discovery and parsing - Inspect the input/sites/ directory tree and process all .html files you find. - These pages use three structures you should handle: a) Articles:
with a

title, a date, optional , optional link, and a

. b) Lists:

    with
  • items containing YYYY-MM-DD, , optional
    , and optional . c) Tables: where each data row has columns [Date, Title, Speaker, Venue, Link]. 2) Filtering and name matching - Keep only records that mention Mr. Ma Hanbao by name, matching either "Ma Hanbao" or "Hanbao Ma" (case-insensitive) in any of: title, description/summary, or (for tables) the Speaker column. - For every kept record, set the people field to "Ma Hanbao" exactly. 3) Fields to extract for each kept record - date: ISO format YYYY-MM-DD taken from the , the text, or the Date column in the table. - title: from

    , , or the Title column. - location: from or the Venue column if present, else leave empty. - link: from an associated with the item (the article’s link, or the Link column if present), else leave empty. - source_file: the relative path of the HTML file the record came from (e.g., input/sites/univ_vienna/news_2016.html). 4) Deduplication and ordering - Deduplicate exact duplicates by (lowercased, trimmed title + date). When duplicates occur, process files in lexicographic order by their relative path and keep the first encountered record. - Produce a consolidated CSV sorted by date ascending. Deliverables (create these exact files): - output/hanbao_events.csv — UTF-8 CSV with header: date,title,people,location,link,source_file. Include only the filtered and deduplicated records sorted by date ascending. - output/top_by_recency.json — an array of the 3 most recent records (by date descending) from the CSV; include objects with keys: date, title, source_file. - output/scan_log.txt — one line per processed HTML file in the format: ": total=, matched=". Notes: - Treat matching as case-insensitive for the two name variants only ("Ma Hanbao", "Hanbao Ma"). - Leave fields empty if not present in the source. I’m a retired professor from the University of Vienna and Ma Hanbao is an old friend from our teaching days; this is for a small keepsake. Thank you for making the outputs deterministic and placing them under the output/ folder as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
